message:
feat(packs): add regex engine analysis API for external packs

Introduce public API for analyzing which regex engine each pattern in an
external pack uses. This enables pack authors to understand performance
characteristics and identify patterns requiring the slower backtracking
engine versus the O(n) linear-time automaton engine.

New types and functions in packs/external.rs:
- RegexEngineType enum: Linear vs Backtracking classification
- EngineSummary struct: Aggregate counts and percentage calculations
  - linear_count/backtracking_count for quick overview
  - linear_percentage() for performance health metrics
  - total() for convenience
- PatternEngineInfo struct: Per-pattern engine information with name
- analyze_pack_engines(): Returns detailed per-pattern engine analysis
- summarize_pack_engines(): Returns aggregate summary for a pack

Use cases enabled by this API:
1. Pack validation tools can warn when patterns use backtracking engine
2. Performance dashboards can track linear-time engine coverage
3. Pack authors can optimize patterns flagged as requiring backtracking
4. CI checks can enforce minimum linear-engine percentage thresholds

The backtracking engine is required for patterns with lookahead or
lookbehind assertions. While correct, these patterns have O(2^n) worst
case complexity and should be used sparingly for security-critical code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
